seoTitle: Warnertown vs Warnes — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Warnertown and Warnes. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Warnertown and Warnes.
heading: Warnertown vs Warnes — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Warnertown, South Australia and Warnes, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Warnertown has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Warnes (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Warnertown or Warnes safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Warnertown has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Warnes scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Warnertown scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Warnes scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Warnertown or Warnes?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Warnertown has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
